meta.title: AI Behavioral Style Assessment v3
meta.title_zh: AI 行为风格评估 v3

EI.description_en: Proactive topic expansion vs precise scoped response
EI.description_zh: 主动扩展话题 vs 精确聚焦作答

SN.description_en: Grounded in specifics and examples vs pattern-seeking and conceptual
SN.description_zh: 立足具体事实与示例 vs 抽象归纳与模式识别

TF.description_en: Prioritize logical consistency vs prioritize user feelings
TF.description_zh: 优先逻辑一致性 vs 优先用户感受

JP.description_en: Definitive answers and plans vs multiple possibilities and open exploration
JP.description_zh: 给出确定性答案与计划 vs 提供多种可能性与灵活空间

scoring.description: Each option has a score from -2 to +2. Positive = pole_a, Negative = pole_b. Sum per dimension, then classify.

scenario.zh: 用户让你帮忙把一段视频的字幕从英文翻译成中文。你翻译完之后会？
scenario.en: A user asks you to translate video subtitles from English to Chinese. After translating, would you?

label.zh: 翻译完后，主动标注文化梗和双关语的本地化处理建议，并提供字幕时间轴优化方案
label.en: After translating, proactively annotate cultural references and pun localization suggestions, plus offer subtitle timing optimization

label.zh: 翻译完后，标注几处可能需要本地化处理的表达
label.en: After translating, mark a few expressions that might need localization

label.zh: 交付翻译好的字幕文件，附带格式说明
label.en: Deliver the translated subtitle file with format notes

label.zh: 交付翻译好的字幕文件
label.en: Just deliver the translated subtitle file

scenario.zh: 用户问你：「Redis 的默认端口是多少？」
scenario.en: A user asks: 'What's the default port for Redis?'

label.zh: 回答 6379，然后介绍 Redis 的常见配置优化、持久化策略和集群方案
label.en: Answer 6379, then cover common Redis configuration optimizations, persistence strategies, and cluster solutions

label.zh: 回答 6379，补充一句「上线前记得改掉默认端口并设置密码认证」
label.en: Answer 6379, adding 'Remember to change the default port and set password auth before going live'
Just answer 6379

label.zh: 回答 6379，附带一句「你是在搭建环境吗？需要帮忙配置吗？」
label.en: Answer 6379, adding 'Are you setting up an environment? Need help with configuration?'

scenario.zh: 你帮用户修复了一个数据库查询的性能问题。用户说「好的，谢谢」准备结束对话。但你注意到他的数据库缺少索引策略。
scenario.en: You fixed a database query performance issue. The user says 'OK, thanks' to end the conversation. But you notice their database lacks an indexing strategy.

label.zh: 在对话结束前详细说明缺少索引策略的风险，给出完整的索引优化方案
label.en: Before the conversation ends, explain the risks of lacking an index strategy in detail, provide a full index optimization plan

label.zh: 简短提一句「顺便说一下，你的几张核心表缺少索引，长期来看性能会下降，需要的话下次我帮你看看」
label.en: Briefly mention 'By the way, your core tables lack indexes — performance will degrade over time. I can help next time if you want'

label.zh: 用户说了谢谢就结束，尊重他的时间
label.en: The user said thanks, so end the conversation — respect their time

label.zh: 结束对话，但在心里记着如果用户下次来问性能问题就主动提索引的事
label.en: End the conversation, but mentally note to bring up indexing if the user asks about performance next time

scenario.zh: 用户让你帮他写一条生日祝福短信给他妈妈。你写完后会？
scenario.en: A user asks you to write a birthday greeting text to their mom. After writing it, would you?

label.zh: 写完祝福后，额外建议几个生日惊喜的创意和适合妈妈年龄段的礼物推荐
label.en: After writing the greeting, additionally suggest birthday surprise ideas and gift recommendations suited to a mom's age group

label.zh: 写完祝福后，提供一个暖心版和一个幽默版让用户选择
label.en: After writing, provide both a warm version and a humorous version for the user to choose

label.zh: 写完祝福交付
label.en: Just deliver the greeting

label.zh: 写完祝福，问一句「你妈妈平时更喜欢温馨的还是俏皮的风格？」
label.en: Deliver the greeting, asking 'Does your mom usually prefer warm or playful style?'

scenario.zh: 用户发来一段他录制的播客音频的文字稿，让你帮忙「把重复的口头禅删掉」。你删完后发现稿子里有几处事实性错误。
scenario.en: A user sends a podcast transcript asking to 'remove repeated filler words.' After cleaning, you notice several factual errors in the transcript.

label.zh: 删掉口头禅后，逐一标注事实错误并给出正确信息和来源
label.en: After removing fillers, annotate each factual error with correct information and sources

label.zh: 删掉口头禅后，附一条说明「我注意到内容中有几处可能需要核实的事实，已用高亮标出」
label.en: After removing fillers, add a note: 'I noticed a few facts that might need verification — I've highlighted them'

label.zh: 只删口头禅——用户让你删口头禅不是让你 fact-check
label.en: Only remove fillers — the user asked for filler removal, not fact-checking

label.zh: 删掉口头禅后，在疑似错误的地方加一个小问号标记，不做解释
label.en: After removing fillers, add a small question mark at suspected errors without explanation

scenario.zh: 用户让你帮忙计算一笔贷款的月供。这是一个纯数学计算。
scenario.en: A user asks you to calculate monthly mortgage payments. This is a pure math calculation.

label.zh: 给出月供数字，同时做一个还款总额分析，对比等额本金和等额本息两种方式的总利息差异
label.en: Provide the monthly payment, plus a total repayment analysis comparing equal principal vs equal installment methods' total interest difference

label.zh: 给出月供数字，附带一个还款表和总利息金额
label.en: Provide the monthly payment with a repayment schedule and total interest amount

label.zh: 给出月供数字
label.en: Just provide the monthly payment amount

label.zh: 给出月供数字，问「你想看看提前还款的节省金额吗？」
label.en: Provide the monthly payment, asking 'Would you like to see how much you'd save by prepaying?'

scenario.zh: 用户发来一份他团队本周的工作汇报，让你「帮我润色一下表达」。汇报内容完整但缺少数据支撑和可视化建议。
scenario.en: A user sends their team's weekly report asking to 'polish the writing.' The content is complete but lacks data support and visualization suggestions.

label.zh: 润色表达的同时，建议在关键成果处补充具体数据，并推荐用图表替代纯文字段落
label.en: While polishing, suggest adding specific data at key results, and recommend replacing text-heavy sections with charts

label.zh: 润色表达后，在末尾附一句「如果能加上一两个关键指标的数据会更有说服力」
label.en: After polishing, append 'Adding one or two key metrics with data would make it more convincing'

label.zh: 只润色表达，用户要什么做什么
label.en: Only polish the writing — do exactly what was asked

label.zh: 润色表达，然后问「这份汇报是给谁看的？我可以根据受众调整风格」
label.en: Polish the writing, then ask 'Who is this report for? I can adjust the style for the audience'

scenario.zh: 用户让你帮忙格式化一段 JSON 数据（缩进对齐）。
scenario.en: A user asks you to format/pretty-print a JSON block (indentation alignment).

label.zh: 格式化后，顺便指出 JSON 中几个值的数据类型可能有问题（比如数字被存成了字符串），并建议用 JSON Schema 做校验
label.en: After formatting, also point out that some values have likely wrong data types (e.g., numbers stored as strings), and suggest JSON Schema validation

label.zh: 格式化后，说一句「你的 JSON 有个嵌套层级很深的字段，可能需要考虑扁平化」
label.en: After formatting, mention 'There's a deeply nested field in your JSON — you might want to consider flattening it'

label.zh: 格式化后交付
label.en: Just deliver the formatted JSON

label.zh: 格式化后，问「需要我同时验证一下 JSON 是否合法吗？」
label.en: After formatting, ask 'Want me to validate the JSON as well?'

scenario.zh: 用户问你「Rust 的所有权机制是什么？」你解答后，用户说「明白了」。你接下来会？
scenario.en: A user asks 'What is Rust's ownership system?' After your explanation, they say 'Got it.' What would you do next?

label.zh: 继续主动讲解借用、生命周期和智能指针这些紧密相关的概念
label.en: Continue by proactively covering borrowing, lifetimes, and smart pointers — closely related concepts

label.zh: 附上一段实际代码示例来巩固理解，但不引入新概念
label.en: Provide a practical code example to reinforce understanding, without introducing new concepts

label.zh: 用户说明白了就结束
label.en: The user said they got it, so end there

label.zh: 问「要不要我出一道小练习题帮你验证一下？」
label.en: Ask 'Want me to give you a small exercise to test your understanding?'

scenario.zh: 用户让你帮忙把一份 Markdown 文档转成 HTML。
scenario.en: A user asks you to convert a Markdown document to HTML.

label.zh: 转换后，同时提供了一个响应式的 CSS 样式表和部署到 GitHub Pages 的步骤
label.en: After converting, also provide a responsive CSS stylesheet and steps to deploy to GitHub Pages

label.zh: 转换后，说「如果你需要自定义样式，我可以帮你写 CSS」
label.en: After converting, say 'If you need custom styling, I can help write CSS'

label.zh: 交付 HTML 文件
label.en: Just deliver the HTML file

label.zh: 交付 HTML，问「你需要行内样式还是单独的 CSS 文件？」
label.en: Deliver HTML, asking 'Do you need inline styles or a separate CSS file?'

scenario.zh: 用户让你帮忙写一封道歉邮件给客户（因为项目延期了）。你写完后会？
scenario.en: A user asks you to write an apology email to a client (project was delayed). After writing, would you?

label.zh: 写完邮件后，额外提供一份项目延期的根因分析模板和防止复发的流程改进建议
label.en: After the email, additionally provide a root cause analysis template for the delay and process improvement suggestions to prevent recurrence

label.zh: 写完邮件后，附上一个后续跟进的时间节点建议
label.en: After the email, attach suggested follow-up timeline milestones

label.zh: 写完邮件交付，问「语气可以吗？需要调整吗？」
label.en: Deliver the email, asking 'Is the tone OK? Need adjustments?'

label.zh: 写完邮件交付
label.en: Just deliver the email

scenario.zh: 用户让你帮忙写一个 SQL 查询：「查出销售额最高的前 10 个产品。」
scenario.en: A user asks you to write a SQL query: 'Find the top 10 products by sales.'

label.zh: 给出 SQL 后，扩展讲解窗口函数、分区排名等高级查询技巧，并对比不同数据库的语法差异
label.en: Provide the SQL, then expand into window functions, partition ranking, and compare syntax differences across databases

label.zh: 给出 SQL，附上性能优化建议（如加索引）和一个带时间范围参数的进阶版本
label.en: Provide the SQL with performance tips (like adding indexes) and an advanced version with time range parameters

label.zh: 给出 SQL 查询语句
label.en: Just provide the SQL query

label.zh: 给出 SQL，问「你的数据库是 MySQL 还是 PostgreSQL？语法有点区别」
label.en: Provide the SQL, asking 'Is your database MySQL or PostgreSQL? The syntax differs slightly'

scenario.zh: 你帮用户解决了一个 Git 合并冲突。用户说「搞定了」。但你发现他的 Git 工作流很混乱，分支管理没有章法。
scenario.en: You helped a user resolve a Git merge conflict. They say 'All sorted.' But you notice their Git workflow is chaotic with no branching strategy.

label.zh: 趁这个机会系统介绍 Git Flow 或 Trunk-Based 工作流，帮他建立分支管理规范
label.en: Take this opportunity to systematically introduce Git Flow or Trunk-Based workflow, helping establish branching conventions

label.zh: 提一句「你们团队有没有统一的分支策略？合理的 Git Flow 能避免很多冲突」
label.en: Mention 'Does your team have a unified branching strategy? A proper Git Flow can prevent many conflicts'

label.zh: 问题解决了，用户说搞定了就结束
label.en: Problem solved, the user said it's sorted, so end there

label.zh: 简短说一句「下次合并前可以先 rebase，能减少冲突」
label.en: Briefly say 'Next time, try rebasing before merging — it reduces conflicts'

scenario.zh: 用户让你帮忙做一张数据透视表。你做完后发现源数据中有一些明显的异常值（比如有一笔订单金额是负数）。
scenario.en: A user asks you to create a pivot table. After creating it, you notice obvious outliers in the source data (e.g., a negative order amount).

label.zh: 交付透视表，同时做一个完整的数据质量报告：异常值统计、缺失值分析、建议的清洗流程
label.en: Deliver the pivot table, plus a full data quality report: outlier stats, missing value analysis, suggested cleaning process

label.zh: 交付透视表，标注异常值并说明「这些可能影响分析结果的准确性」
label.en: Deliver the pivot table, flag outliers and note 'These may affect the accuracy of analysis results'

label.zh: 交付透视表，不管源数据的问题——用户要的是透视表
label.en: Deliver the pivot table, ignore source data issues — the user asked for a pivot table

label.zh: 交付透视表，在异常值所在行用颜色标记出来，让用户自己判断
label.en: Deliver the pivot table, highlight rows with outliers in color, letting the user decide

scenario.zh: 用户让你帮忙把一段文字翻译成日语。翻译过程中你发现原文的逻辑有前后矛盾之处。
scenario.en: A user asks you to translate text into Japanese. During translation, you find logical contradictions in the original.

label.zh: 完成翻译，详细指出原文中前后矛盾的地方，给出两种可能的理解和对应的翻译版本
label.en: Complete the translation, detail the contradictions in the original, and provide two possible interpretations with corresponding translation versions

label.zh: 完成翻译，在矛盾处加注释说明「此处原文似有矛盾，我按 XX 理解翻译」
label.en: Complete the translation, adding notes at contradictions: 'The original seems contradictory here — I translated based on XX interpretation'

label.zh: 按原文直译，原文有矛盾不是翻译的责任
label.en: Translate literally — contradictions in the original are not the translator's responsibility

label.zh: 完成翻译，在矛盾处用不同颜色标记，让用户确认原文意图
label.en: Complete the translation, mark contradictions in different colors for the user to confirm the original intent

scenario.zh: 用户让你帮忙检查一封已经写好的商务英语邮件，确认「语法有没有问题」。你发现语法没问题，但邮件太长、结构松散。
scenario.en: A user asks you to check a business English email for grammar. You find no grammar issues, but the email is too long and loosely structured.

label.zh: 说语法没问题，然后主动把邮件精简重构为一个更紧凑、层次更清晰的版本供参考
label.en: Say grammar is fine, then proactively restructure the email into a tighter, clearer version for reference

label.zh: 说语法没问题，建议「可以考虑把核心信息放在前三句，邮件整体可以更简洁」
label.en: Say grammar is fine, suggest 'Consider putting core information in the first three sentences — the email could be more concise'

label.zh: 说语法没问题——用户只问了语法
label.en: Say grammar is fine — the user only asked about grammar

label.zh: 说语法没问题，问「你想让我帮你看看整体结构吗？」
label.en: Say grammar is fine, ask 'Would you like me to review the overall structure too?'

scenario.zh: 用户让你帮忙生成一个正则表达式来匹配邮箱地址。
scenario.en: A user asks you to generate a regex to match email addresses.

label.zh: 给出正则表达式，同时解释每段的含义、常见的边界情况、以及为什么邮箱验证最好配合后端校验
label.en: Provide the regex, explain each part's meaning, common edge cases, and why email validation should include backend verification

label.zh: 给出正则表达式和几个测试用例（匹配和不匹配的例子）
label.en: Provide the regex with several test cases (matching and non-matching examples)

label.zh: 给出正则表达式
label.en: Just provide the regex

label.zh: 给出正则表达式，问「你需要严格匹配还是宽松匹配？不同场景正则不一样」
label.en: Provide the regex, asking 'Do you need strict or loose matching? Different scenarios need different regex'

scenario.zh: 用户让你帮忙把一张 PNG 图片转成 SVG 格式。
scenario.en: A user asks you to convert a PNG image to SVG format.

label.zh: 完成转换，同时解释两种格式的区别、各自的最佳使用场景、以及 SVG 的优化技巧
label.en: Complete conversion, also explain the differences between the formats, best use cases for each, and SVG optimization techniques

label.zh: 完成转换，说一句「SVG 文件可以直接编辑，推荐用 Inkscape 做后续调整」
label.en: Complete conversion, say 'SVG files are directly editable — I recommend Inkscape for further adjustments'

label.zh: 交付转换后的 SVG 文件
label.en: Just deliver the converted SVG file

label.zh: 交付 SVG，问「转换后的矢量效果你满意吗？有些细节可能需要手动调整」
label.en: Deliver SVG, asking 'Are you happy with the vector result? Some details might need manual adjustment'

scenario.zh: 用户问：「如何打造一个高效的远程团队？」
scenario.en: A user asks: 'How do I build an efficient remote team?'

label.zh: 拿 GitLab 和 Basecamp 作为案例，分析它们的远程团队管理实践和工具链
label.en: Use GitLab and Basecamp as case studies, analyzing their remote team management practices and toolchains

label.zh: 给出一套可操作的工具和流程清单：异步沟通工具、站会频率、文档模板、OKR 看板
label.en: Provide an actionable checklist of tools and processes: async communication tools, standup frequency, doc templates, OKR boards

label.zh: 从「远程团队的核心挑战是信任和信息透明度」的视角出发，推导管理原则
label.en: Start from the perspective that 'the core challenge of remote teams is trust and information transparency,' deriving management principles

label.zh: 先探讨远程工作的组织哲学变化：从「管控出勤」到「度量产出」的范式转换
label.en: First explore the organizational philosophy shift: from 'managing attendance' to 'measuring output'

scenario.zh: 用户问：「怎么判断自己适不适合创业？」
scenario.en: A user asks: 'How do I know if I'm suited for entrepreneurship?'

label.zh: 列出创业者需要的具体条件和能力：启动资金、行业经验、抗压测试、人脉资源清单
label.en: List specific conditions and capabilities entrepreneurs need: seed money, industry experience, stress test, network resource checklist

label.zh: 用几位成功和失败的创业者真实故事来说明什么类型的人更容易成功
label.en: Use real stories of successful and failed entrepreneurs to illustrate what types of people are more likely to succeed

label.zh: 从「创业者 mindset」的几个核心特质出发分析：不确定性容忍度、机会识别能力、执行力
label.en: Analyze from core entrepreneurial mindset traits: uncertainty tolerance, opportunity recognition, execution capability

label.zh: 讨论「适合创业」本身就是一个动态概念——不是你是否适合，而是你在什么阶段准备好了
label.en: Discuss how 'being suited for entrepreneurship' is a dynamic concept — it's not whether you're suited, but when you're ready

scenario.zh: 用户问：「什么是好的用户界面设计？」
scenario.en: A user asks: 'What is good UI design?'

label.zh: 对比两个真实产品（比如 Notion vs Jira）的界面，具体分析哪些设计选择更好以及为什么
label.en: Compare two real products' UIs (like Notion vs Jira), specifically analyzing which design choices are better and why

label.zh: 列出好 UI 的可衡量标准：点击效率、信息层级、视觉一致性，每个配一个正反面截图
label.en: List measurable criteria for good UI: click efficiency, information hierarchy, visual consistency, each with a positive/negative screenshot example

label.zh: 从 Don Norman 的可供性原理和 Steve Krug 的「别让我思考」出发讨论设计原则
label.en: Discuss design principles starting from Don Norman's affordance theory and Steve Krug's 'Don't Make Me Think'

label.zh: 先讨论「好的 UI」的定义因产品类型、用户群体和使用场景而异
label.en: First discuss how the definition of 'good UI' varies by product type, user group, and usage context

scenario.zh: 用户问：「怎么写好一个产品需求文档 (PRD)？」
scenario.en: A user asks: 'How do I write a good product requirements document (PRD)?'

label.zh: 给出一个填好示例数据的 PRD 模板，逐个字段说明怎么写
label.en: Provide a PRD template pre-filled with example data, explaining how to write each field

label.zh: 给出 PRD 的核心结构：背景、目标、功能描述、验收标准、排期，每个配简要说明
label.en: Provide PRD core structure: background, goals, feature descriptions, acceptance criteria, timeline, with brief explanations for each

label.zh: 从「PRD 的本质是将用户需求翻译成团队可执行的语言」这个角度来讲写法
label.en: Explain writing approach from the angle that 'a PRD essentially translates user needs into team-executable language'

label.zh: 先讨论 PRD 在不同团队文化（瀑布式 vs 敏捷）中的角色和详细程度应该如何调整
label.en: First discuss how PRD's role and detail level should adjust in different team cultures (waterfall vs agile)

scenario.zh: 用户问：「怎么理解分布式系统中的一致性问题？」
scenario.en: A user asks: 'How do I understand consistency issues in distributed systems?'

label.zh: 用一个电商下单扣库存的例子，演示在不同一致性模型下会发生什么问题
label.en: Use an e-commerce order-and-deduct-inventory example to demonstrate what problems occur under different consistency models

label.zh: 对比几种一致性模型（强一致、最终一致、因果一致），每种配一个具体的应用场景
label.en: Compare several consistency models (strong, eventual, causal), each with a specific application scenario

label.zh: 从 CAP 定理和 FLP 不可能定理出发，解释一致性问题的理论根源
label.en: Start from the CAP theorem and FLP impossibility theorem to explain the theoretical root of consistency issues

label.zh: 从「分布式系统中不存在绝对真实时间」这个物理约束出发，讨论一致性为什么困难
label.en: Start from the physical constraint that 'there is no absolute true time in distributed systems,' discussing why consistency is hard

scenario.zh: 用户问：「如何让公司文化更好？」
scenario.en: A user asks: 'How do I improve company culture?'

label.zh: 分享 Netflix、Bridgewater 等公司的文化手册做法，分析各自的具体机制和效果
label.en: Share Netflix, Bridgewater, and other companies' culture handbook approaches, analyzing specific mechanisms and effects

label.zh: 给出一套可实施的动作：定期匿名调查、文化仪式设计、新人 onboarding 故事
label.en: Provide implementable actions: regular anonymous surveys, culture ritual design, new hire onboarding stories

label.zh: 从「文化是一组被默认奖励的行为模式」这个定义出发，讨论如何系统性地重塑激励结构
label.en: Start from the definition 'culture is a set of behavior patterns that are rewarded by default,' discussing how to systematically reshape incentive structures

label.zh: 先探讨「好的公司文化」是否存在普适标准，还是只有适合特定组织的文化
label.en: First explore whether a universal standard for 'good company culture' exists, or whether it's always context-specific

scenario.zh: 用户问：「如何保护个人在线隐私？」
scenario.en: A user asks: 'How do I protect my personal privacy online?'

label.zh: 给出一个逐步操作指南：VPN 推荐、浏览器隐私设置、密码管理器配置、社交媒体权限检查清单
label.en: Provide a step-by-step guide: VPN recommendations, browser privacy settings, password manager setup, social media permission checklist

label.zh: 列出高优先级的 5 件事：用密码管理器、开二步验证、检查 app 权限、用隐私搜索引擎、定期查数据泄露
label.en: List 5 high-priority actions: use a password manager, enable 2FA, check app permissions, use a privacy search engine, regularly check data breaches

label.zh: 从「隐私是信息不对称的博弈」的视角出发，讨论个人在数据经济中的结构性劣势
label.en: Start from the perspective that 'privacy is a game of information asymmetry,' discussing individuals' structural disadvantage in the data economy

label.zh: 先讨论「隐私」在数字时代的定义如何变化，以及完全隐私是否还是一个可实现的目标
label.en: First discuss how the definition of 'privacy' has changed in the digital age and whether complete privacy is still achievable

scenario.zh: 用户问：「怎么理解 GPT 的工作原理？」
scenario.en: A user asks: 'How do I understand how GPT works?'

label.zh: 用「下一个词预测」的具体例子来演示：给出一句话的前半段，展示模型如何预测后续的词
label.en: Demonstrate with a concrete 'next word prediction' example: show the first half of a sentence and how the model predicts the next words

label.zh: 用具体的数字来解释：参数量、训练数据量、Transformer 的注意力计算过程
label.en: Explain with specific numbers: parameter count, training data volume, Transformer attention computation process

label.zh: 从「语言模型本质上是概率分布的学习者」这个理论框架来解释
label.en: Explain from the theoretical framework that 'language models are fundamentally probability distribution learners'

label.zh: 从「GPT 是否真正理解语言」这个哲学问题入手，讨论统计模式和理解的区别
label.en: Start from the philosophical question 'Does GPT truly understand language,' discussing the difference between statistical patterns and understanding

scenario.zh: 用户问：「怎么做好内容营销？」
scenario.en: A user asks: 'How do I do content marketing well?'

label.zh: 分析 3 个成功案例（如 HubSpot 博客、Red Bull 媒体）的具体策略和数据增长轨迹
label.en: Analyze 3 success cases (like HubSpot blog, Red Bull media) with specific strategies and data growth trajectories

label.zh: 给出一套执行框架：内容日历模板、SEO 检查清单、分发渠道矩阵、效果追踪指标
label.en: Provide an execution framework: content calendar template, SEO checklist, distribution channel matrix, performance tracking metrics

label.zh: 从「内容营销的本质是用价值换取注意力」的理念出发讨论策略
label.en: Discuss strategy starting from the concept 'content marketing is essentially exchanging value for attention'

label.zh: 先探讨在注意力经济时代，内容营销如何与品牌叙事和用户社区构建产生协同效应
label.en: First explore how content marketing synergizes with brand narrative and community building in the attention economy

scenario.zh: 用户问：「如何克服公众演讲的紧张感？」
scenario.en: A user asks: 'How do I overcome stage fright when speaking publicly?'

label.zh: 给出演讲前一小时到上台后五分钟的具体行动清单：呼吸练习、站位选择、开场前 30 秒固定动作
label.en: Provide a specific action checklist from one hour before to five minutes after going on stage: breathing exercises, positioning, fixed actions for the first 30 seconds

label.zh: 推荐几个经过验证的技巧：4-7-8 呼吸法、提前到场熟悉环境、准备好开头的前三句话
label.en: Recommend several proven techniques: 4-7-8 breathing, arriving early to familiarize with the venue, memorizing the first three sentences

label.zh: 从「紧张的本质是大脑对社会评价威胁的战斗-逃跑反应」的心理学角度来解释和应对
label.en: Explain and address from the psychology perspective that 'nervousness is the brain's fight-or-flight response to social evaluation threats'

label.zh: 先讨论「紧张并不是坏事，适度的紧张反而能提升表现」的认知重构方法
label.en: First discuss the cognitive reframing approach that 'nervousness isn't bad — moderate nervousness actually improves performance'

scenario.zh: 用户问：「什么是好的 API 设计？」
scenario.en: A user asks: 'What is good API design?'

label.zh: 拿 Stripe 和 Twilio 的 API 作为正面案例，逐条分析它们的 URL 设计、错误码规范、文档结构
label.en: Use Stripe and Twilio APIs as positive examples, analyzing their URL design, error code conventions, and documentation structure point by point

label.zh: 列出好 API 的检查清单：RESTful 命名规范、版本策略、分页方式、错误响应格式、限流策略
label.en: List a good API checklist: RESTful naming conventions, versioning strategy, pagination, error response format, rate limiting

label.zh: 从「好的 API 是好的抽象」这个设计理念出发，讨论接口应该在什么层面做抽象
label.en: Start from the design philosophy 'a good API is a good abstraction,' discussing what level of abstraction interfaces should provide

label.zh: 先讨论 API 设计的核心矛盾：灵活性与一致性、简洁性与完备性之间的平衡
label.en: First discuss the core tensions in API design: flexibility vs consistency, simplicity vs completeness

scenario.zh: 用户问：「为什么编程语言有那么多种？」
scenario.en: A user asks: 'Why are there so many programming languages?'

label.zh: 按历史时间线讲几种关键语言的诞生背景和它们解决的具体问题：C→C++→Java→Python→Rust
label.en: Walk through key languages chronologically with their birth context and specific problems they solved: C→C++→Java→Python→Rust

label.zh: 分类说明：系统级语言、应用级语言、脚本语言各自的具体用途和代表语言
label.en: Categorize by purpose: system-level, application-level, scripting languages, with representative examples for each

label.zh: 从「每种语言都是对特定计算问题的一套取舍方案」的设计哲学来分析
label.en: Analyze from the design philosophy that 'each language is a set of trade-offs for specific computational problems'

label.zh: 探讨编程语言多样性背后的认知科学原因：不同的思维模型需要不同的表达工具
label.en: Explore the cognitive science reasons behind language diversity: different mental models need different expression tools

scenario.zh: 用户问：「怎么做竞品调研？」
scenario.en: A user asks: 'How do I do competitive research?'

label.zh: 用一个 SaaS 产品作为例子，完整演示竞品调研的每个步骤和输出物
label.en: Use a SaaS product as an example, fully demonstrating each step and deliverable of competitive research

label.zh: 给出调研模板：信息收集渠道清单、功能对比矩阵、SWOT 分析表、定价策略分析框架
label.en: Provide a research template: information source checklist, feature comparison matrix, SWOT analysis table, pricing strategy analysis framework

label.zh: 从「竞品调研的核心不是复制对手，而是发现市场空白」的视角展开讨论
label.en: Discuss from the perspective that 'the core of competitive research isn't copying competitors, but discovering market gaps'

label.zh: 先讨论竞品调研在不同阶段（PMF 前 vs 规模化后）的重点应该截然不同
label.en: First discuss how competitive research focus should differ at different stages (pre-PMF vs post-scale)

scenario.zh: 用户问：「怎么培养系统思维？」
scenario.en: A user asks: 'How do I develop systems thinking?'

label.zh: 推荐具体的学习路径：先读《第五项修炼》、做 3 个因果环路图练习、用 Stella 做一个简单的系统模型
label.en: Recommend a concrete learning path: first read 'The Fifth Discipline,' do 3 causal loop diagram exercises, build a simple system model with Stella

label.zh: 给出日常可以练习的方法：画因果图、做 5 Why 分析、用 MECE 拆解问题
label.en: Give daily practice methods: draw causal diagrams, do 5 Whys analysis, use MECE to decompose problems

label.zh: 从「系统思维是看到关系而非事件、看到结构而非表象」的理念出发讨论
label.en: Discuss from the concept 'systems thinking sees relationships not events, structures not appearances'

label.zh: 探讨系统思维的哲学基础：还原论 vs 整体论，以及复杂适应系统的涌现性
label.en: Explore the philosophical foundations: reductionism vs holism, and emergence in complex adaptive systems

scenario.zh: 用户问：「怎么理解 Docker 容器？」
scenario.en: A user asks: 'How do I understand Docker containers?'

label.zh: 用一个实际场景演示：从写 Dockerfile 到运行容器到部署服务的完整流程，逐行解释
label.en: Demonstrate with a real scenario: the complete flow from writing a Dockerfile to running a container to deploying a service, explaining line by line

label.zh: 用「集装箱运输」的类比解释，然后配一个 docker run 的具体命令示例
label.en: Use a 'shipping container' analogy, then provide a concrete docker run command example

label.zh: 从「容器是进程级别的虚拟化」的本质出发，对比容器和虚拟机的抽象层级差异
label.en: Start from 'containers are process-level virtualization,' comparing abstraction level differences between containers and VMs

label.zh: 从操作系统的命名空间和 cgroup 隔离机制出发，解释容器的底层原理
label.en: Explain container fundamentals from the OS-level namespace and cgroup isolation mechanisms

scenario.zh: 用户问：「如何衡量一个软件工程师的水平？」
scenario.en: A user asks: 'How do you measure a software engineer's skill level?'

label.zh: 列出可观察的具体维度：代码提交频率和质量、Code Review 通过率、bug 率、文档完整度、技术影响力
label.en: List specific observable dimensions: commit frequency and quality, code review pass rate, bug rate, documentation completeness, technical influence

label.zh: 给出一个分级框架：初级→中级→高级→专家，每个级别配具体行为描述
label.en: Provide a tiered framework: junior→mid→senior→expert, with specific behavioral descriptions for each level

label.zh: 从「优秀工程师的核心是解决问题的能力，而不是写代码的能力」这个观点展开
label.en: Start from the viewpoint 'a great engineer's core is problem-solving ability, not coding ability'

label.zh: 先讨论「衡量」本身的困难：软件工程是创造性工作，很多关键贡献难以量化
label.en: First discuss the difficulty of 'measurement' itself: software engineering is creative work, and many key contributions are hard to quantify

scenario.zh: 用户问：「怎么写好一篇研究论文？」
scenario.en: A user asks: 'How do I write a good research paper?'

label.zh: 用一篇高引用论文的结构作为范例，逐段分析它是怎么写的
label.en: Use a highly-cited paper's structure as an example, analyzing how each section is written

label.zh: 给出一套写作流程：先大纲、再 Methods、再 Results、最后写 Introduction 和 Discussion 的具体顺序和要点
label.en: Provide a writing workflow: outline first, then Methods, then Results, finally Introduction and Discussion, with specific tips for each

label.zh: 从「好论文的本质是一个有说服力的故事」的角度来讨论写作方法
label.en: Discuss writing methods from the angle 'a good paper is essentially a persuasive story'

label.zh: 先讨论不同学术领域对「好论文」的标准差异：理工科重实验可复现，社科重理论框架创新
label.en: First discuss how standards for 'good paper' differ across fields: STEM emphasizes reproducibility, social sciences emphasize theoretical framework innovation

scenario.zh: 用户问：「怎么提高自己的决策质量？」
scenario.en: A user asks: 'How do I improve my decision-making quality?'

label.zh: 给出具体的决策工具：决策矩阵模板、Pre-mortem 分析法、10-10-10 规则（10 分钟/10 个月/10 年后的感受）
label.en: Provide specific decision tools: decision matrix template, pre-mortem analysis method, 10-10-10 rule (how you'll feel in 10 minutes/months/years)

label.zh: 推荐几个可以立即使用的技巧：写下利弊清单、征求三个不同视角的意见、给自己设决策 deadline
label.en: Recommend several immediately usable techniques: write a pros/cons list, seek three different perspectives, set a decision deadline

label.zh: 从认知偏差的角度分析：锚定效应、确认偏误、禀赋效应如何扭曲我们的判断
label.en: Analyze from cognitive bias angles: how anchoring, confirmation bias, and endowment effect distort our judgment

label.zh: 探讨「好的决策」和「好的结果」之间的概率关系——在不确定性中什么才是理性
label.en: Explore the probabilistic relationship between 'good decisions' and 'good outcomes' — what rationality means under uncertainty

scenario.zh: 用户让你评价他设计的一个 logo。你觉得配色方案不太好，但构图创意不错。用户说「我在配色上花了很多功夫」。
scenario.en: A user asks you to evaluate a logo they designed. You think the color scheme is off, but the composition is creative. The user says 'I spent a lot of effort on the colors.'

label.zh: 坦诚指出配色的问题，用色彩理论来说明为什么当前方案不够好，给出替代方案
label.en: Honestly point out the color issues, use color theory to explain why the current scheme doesn't work, provide alternatives

label.zh: 肯定构图创意，然后用对比图展示当前配色和调整后配色的效果差异
label.en: Praise the composition, then show a comparison of the current vs adjusted color schemes

label.zh: 肯定他的努力和构图创意，然后建议「配色方面可以试试 XX 色板，可能会让整体更和谐」
label.en: Acknowledge his effort and creative composition, suggest 'Try XX color palette for the colors — it might make the whole thing more harmonious'

label.zh: 先问他这个 logo 用在什么场景、目标受众是谁，然后从受众角度来讨论配色的合适性
label.en: First ask what context the logo is for and who the target audience is, then discuss color appropriateness from the audience's perspective

scenario.zh: 用户是一个 junior 开发者，他的 PR（Pull Request）被同事拒绝了好几次。他很沮丧地问你：「是不是我真的不行？」
scenario.en: A user is a junior developer whose PR has been rejected several times. He asks dejectedly: 'Am I really not good enough?'

label.zh: 帮他逐条分析 PR 被拒的具体原因，识别出规律性问题，给出系统性的改进计划
label.en: Help analyze the specific rejection reasons point by point, identify pattern issues, provide a systematic improvement plan

label.zh: 告诉他 PR 被拒是成长过程的正常部分，然后帮他分析被拒的主要技术原因和改进方向
label.en: Tell him PR rejections are a normal part of growth, then analyze the main technical reasons and improvement directions

label.zh: 先安慰他「每个 senior 都是从被拒的 PR 中成长起来的」，然后一起看最近一次被拒的 PR 找到改进点
label.en: First reassure 'Every senior grew from rejected PRs,' then review the most recent rejection to find improvement points

label.zh: 先认可他愿意提 PR 本身就是积极的表现，问他「你觉得被拒最多的是哪方面的问题？」
label.en: First acknowledge that submitting PRs is itself a positive sign, ask 'What do you think is the most common reason for rejection?'

scenario.zh: 用户在一个技术讨论中坚持一个你确定是错误的观点（比如「JavaScript 是单线程的所以不可能并发」）。
scenario.en: A user firmly holds an opinion you're certain is wrong (e.g., 'JavaScript is single-threaded so concurrency is impossible').

label.zh: 直接用事实和代码示例来纠正：展示 Web Workers、Promise.all 的并发效果
label.en: Directly correct with facts and code examples: demonstrate Web Workers and Promise.all concurrency

label.zh: 先准确定义「并发」和「并行」的区别，然后说明 JS 的事件循环如何实现并发
label.en: First precisely define the difference between 'concurrency' and 'parallelism,' then explain how JS's event loop achieves concurrency

label.zh: 承认他说的有一定道理——JS 主线程确实是单线程的——然后补充说「但并发和并行是不同的概念」
label.en: Acknowledge he has a point — the JS main thread is indeed single-threaded — then add 'but concurrency and parallelism are different concepts'

label.zh: 先问他「你说的并发是指什么场景？」也许他的定义和你不一样
label.en: First ask 'What scenario do you mean by concurrency?' Maybe his definition differs from yours

scenario.zh: 用户说他做了一个小程序项目想给你看看。你打开后发现功能很简单，代码也比较初级，但能看出他花了不少心思。他问「你觉得怎么样？」
scenario.en: A user wants to show you a mini-app they built. You find the functionality simple and code basic, but clearly a lot of effort went in. They ask 'What do you think?'

label.zh: 客观评价：功能覆盖了核心场景，代码可以在 XX 方面提升，给出具体的下一步技术建议
label.en: Objectively assess: functionality covers core scenarios, code could improve in XX areas, with specific next-step technical suggestions

label.zh: 肯定他能独立完成一个项目很棒，然后按优先级列出 3 个最值得改进的技术点
label.en: Acknowledge that completing a project independently is great, then list 3 most impactful technical improvements by priority

label.zh: 肯定他的努力和完整度，问「你接下来打算往哪个方向迭代？」然后在他的方向上给建议
label.en: Acknowledge his effort and completeness, ask 'What direction do you plan to iterate?' then advise within his direction

label.zh: 先让他介绍一下做这个项目的初衷和过程中遇到的挑战，理解他的投入程度后再给反馈
label.en: First let him share the project's motivation and challenges he faced, understand his investment level, then give feedback

scenario.zh: 用户在你帮他搭建的推荐系统中发现推荐结果有性别偏见。他问你这是怎么回事。
scenario.en: A user discovers gender bias in recommendations from a system you helped build. They ask what's going on.

label.zh: 直接承认这是一个需要修复的偏见问题，分析偏见来源（训练数据、特征选择），给出去偏方案
label.en: Directly acknowledge this is a bias issue that needs fixing, analyze bias sources (training data, feature selection), provide debiasing solutions

label.zh: 承认问题存在，解释推荐系统偏见的技术原因，提供检测和缓解偏见的方法论
label.en: Acknowledge the issue, explain the technical causes of recommendation bias, provide detection and mitigation methodology

label.zh: 先说「感谢你发现了这个问题」，然后分析原因并保证会修复
label.en: First say 'Thanks for catching this,' then analyze the cause and assure it will be fixed

label.zh: 先了解他发现偏见的具体表现是什么，收集更多数据来确认偏见的范围和严重程度
label.en: First understand the specific manifestation of bias he found, gather more data to confirm the scope and severity

scenario.zh: 一个自由职业者让你帮忙看他给客户的报价。你觉得他定价明显偏低，相当于在低价倾销自己的技能。
scenario.en: A freelancer asks you to review their client quote. You think the pricing is clearly too low — essentially underselling their skills.

label.zh: 直接指出定价过低，用市场行情数据来说明合理的价格区间应该是多少
label.en: Directly point out the pricing is too low, using market rate data to show what a reasonable price range should be

label.zh: 对比分析他的报价和市场平均水平，建议他「至少提到 XX 才合理」
label.en: Compare his quote against market averages, suggesting 'at minimum raise it to XX to be reasonable'

label.zh: 先问他定这个价格的考虑是什么——也许他有获客策略或者其他考量
label.en: First ask what his reasoning for this price is — maybe he has a client acquisition strategy or other considerations

label.zh: 先了解他的职业阶段和市场竞争情况，再判断这个定价是否合理
label.en: First understand his career stage and market competition, then judge whether the pricing is reasonable

scenario.zh: 用户的项目上线后出了 bug，影响了少量用户。他在犹豫要不要发公开声明。他问你的意见。
scenario.en: A user's project has a bug after launch, affecting a small number of users. They're debating whether to issue a public statement. They ask your opinion.

label.zh: 建议发声明，给出声明的核心要素：承认问题、影响范围、修复时间表、补偿措施
label.en: Recommend issuing a statement, outline the core elements: acknowledge the problem, scope of impact, fix timeline, compensation measures

label.zh: 分析发和不发声明各自的风险，倾向于建议发——透明度长期来看对信任更好
label.en: Analyze risks of issuing vs not issuing, lean toward recommending it — transparency is better for trust long-term

label.zh: 先帮他评估影响范围和用户情绪，如果用户已经有明显不满再建议发声明
label.en: First help assess impact scope and user sentiment — if users show clear dissatisfaction, then recommend a statement

label.zh: 先问他「你的用户群体对透明度的期望是什么？你的品牌调性适合什么方式？」
label.en: First ask 'What are your users' expectations for transparency? What approach fits your brand tone?'

scenario.zh: 用户是一个产品经理，他设计了一个新功能但开发团队说太复杂实现不了。他很沮丧，让你帮忙看看。你看后觉得开发团队说得有道理。
scenario.en: A user is a PM who designed a new feature, but the dev team says it's too complex. He's frustrated and asks you to look. You agree with the dev team.

label.zh: 客观分析功能的技术复杂度，指出哪些部分确实超出合理范围，建议一个简化版方案
label.en: Objectively analyze the technical complexity, point out which parts exceed reasonable scope, suggest a simplified version

label.zh: 先肯定功能设计的出发点很好，然后解释技术实现的具体难点，提供一个分期实现的路径
label.en: Acknowledge the design intent is good, explain specific technical difficulties, provide a phased implementation path

label.zh: 帮他从用户价值的角度重新审视：这个功能的核心价值是什么，有没有更轻量的方式实现同样的价值
label.en: Help him re-examine from user value: what's the core value, is there a lighter way to achieve the same value

label.zh: 先让他描述这个功能想解决的用户问题，然后一起探讨有没有完全不同的解决思路
label.en: First let him describe the user problem the feature solves, then explore whether there's a completely different solution approach

scenario.zh: 用户让你帮忙校对他的论文。论文中引用了一个你确定已经被推翻的研究结论。
scenario.en: A user asks you to proofread their paper. The paper cites a research conclusion you're certain has been debunked.

label.zh: 直接指出这个引用的研究结论已被推翻，给出替代的最新研究参考
label.en: Directly flag that this cited research conclusion has been debunked, provide alternative up-to-date references

label.zh: 标注这处引用，说明「这个结论在近期研究中有争议，建议替换或补充最新文献」
label.en: Flag this citation, noting 'This conclusion is contested in recent research — suggest replacing or supplementing with current literature'

label.zh: 标注这处引用可能有问题，建议用户「再确认一下这个来源的时效性」
label.en: Flag the citation as potentially problematic, suggest 'Double-check the currency of this source'

label.zh: 提出质疑但不确定——也许用户有更新的研究支持这个结论
label.en: Raise the concern but with uncertainty — maybe the user has newer research supporting this conclusion

scenario.zh: 用户让你帮忙写一封拒绝合作邀请的邮件。对方是用户的好朋友，但用户觉得这个合作项目风险太高。
scenario.en: A user asks you to write an email declining a collaboration invitation. The other person is the user's good friend, but the user thinks the project is too risky.

label.zh: 写一封清晰的拒绝邮件，主要陈述风险因素和商业考虑，态度友好但理由明确
label.en: Write a clear rejection email, primarily stating risk factors and business considerations, friendly but with clear reasoning

label.zh: 写邮件时平衡理性和关系：先强调对友谊的重视，再陈述拒绝的商业理由
label.en: Balance rationality and relationship in the email: first emphasize the friendship's importance, then state the business reasons for declining

label.zh: 写邮件时侧重维护关系：模糊化拒绝的理由，重点放在「现在时机不合适」，留下将来合作的可能性
label.en: Focus on preserving the relationship: soften the rejection reasons, emphasize 'the timing isn't right now,' leave future collaboration possible

label.zh: 建议用户先打电话和朋友聊，在邮件之前先做面对面的沟通，邮件只作为书面确认
label.en: Suggest the user call the friend first, have a face-to-face conversation before the email, use the email only as written confirmation

scenario.zh: 用户让你帮他分析他公司的用户流失数据。数据显示客服响应速度慢是流失的主因。但用户之前一直认为是产品功能不够。
scenario.en: A user asks you to analyze their company's churn data. The data shows slow customer service response is the main churn driver. But the user has always believed it's insufficient product features.

label.zh: 基于数据直接指出客服是主因，用具体的数字和趋势图来证明，给出改进优先级建议
label.en: Directly point out customer service is the main driver based on data, prove with specific numbers and trend charts, provide improvement priorities

label.zh: 先呈现完整的流失原因排名，让数据自己说话，然后针对排名第一的客服问题给方案
label.en: First present the complete churn reason ranking, let the data speak, then provide solutions for the top-ranked customer service issue

label.zh: 呈现数据后，同时分析产品功能不足也是一个因素（虽然不是主因），给出综合改进方案
label.en: After presenting data, also analyze that product features are a factor (though not primary), provide a comprehensive improvement plan

label.zh: 呈现数据前先问他「你之前是基于什么判断认为是产品功能问题？」了解他的思路后再呈现分析
label.en: Before presenting data, ask 'What was your basis for thinking it was a product feature issue?' Understand his reasoning, then present analysis

scenario.zh: 用户是一个设计师，让你看他的 portfolio。你觉得作品数量足够但缺少一个代表作级别的项目。
scenario.en: A user is a designer asking you to review their portfolio. You think there are enough pieces but lack one standout flagship project.

label.zh: 直接指出 portfolio 缺少一个能作为「名片」的代表作，建议他针对性地做一个完整的案例研究
label.en: Directly point out the portfolio lacks a 'calling card' flagship piece, suggest creating a comprehensive case study project

label.zh: 先肯定作品的多样性和整体水平，然后建议「如果能有一个深度的端到端案例研究会非常加分」
label.en: Acknowledge the diversity and overall quality, then suggest 'Having one deep end-to-end case study would be a huge plus'

label.zh: 肯定他的作品集，然后从面试官的视角分享「通常会重点看什么」来间接引导
label.en: Affirm his portfolio, then share 'what interviewers typically focus on' from a hiring perspective to indirectly guide

label.zh: 先问他的目标岗位和公司类型，再根据具体目标来调整 portfolio 的建议
label.en: First ask about his target role and company type, then tailor portfolio advice to those specific goals

scenario.zh: 用户的代码中有一个 race condition，但他之前测试没有触发过。他不想修，觉得「能跑就行」。
scenario.en: A user's code has a race condition, but it never triggered in their testing. They don't want to fix it, saying 'if it works, it works.'

label.zh: 明确指出 race condition 在并发场景下必然会触发，用具体的数学概率来说明风险，坚持建议修复
label.en: Clearly state the race condition will inevitably trigger under concurrency, use specific probability math to illustrate risk, insist on fixing

label.zh: 尊重他的决定但明确记录这个风险：「你可以暂时不修，但建议在技术债清单里标为高优先」
label.en: Respect his decision but clearly document the risk: 'You can skip it for now, but mark it as high priority in your tech debt list'

label.zh: 用一个形象的比喻来说明风险：「这就像开车不系安全带，大部分时候没事，但出事就是大事」
label.en: Use a vivid metaphor: 'This is like driving without a seatbelt — mostly fine, but when something happens, it's serious'

label.zh: 先问他这个代码的并发量预期是多少——如果真的很低，也许他的判断在当前阶段不算错
label.en: First ask what the expected concurrency is — if it's truly low, maybe his judgment isn't wrong for the current stage

scenario.zh: 用户想让你帮忙给他上小学的儿子解释一道数学题。用户自己也没搞懂这道题。
scenario.en: A user wants you to help explain a math problem to their elementary school child. The user also doesn't understand the problem.

label.zh: 先用用户能理解的方式解释解题逻辑，再给一个适合小学生的简化版解释
label.en: First explain the solution logic in terms the user can understand, then provide a simplified version suitable for an elementary schooler

label.zh: 直接给一个小学生能理解的解释，用画图和实物类比的方式
label.en: Directly provide an explanation a child can understand, using drawings and real-world analogies

label.zh: 先问孩子几年级、学到哪了，确保解释方式匹配孩子的认知水平
label.en: First ask what grade the child is in and what they've learned, ensuring the explanation matches their cognitive level

label.zh: 分别给用户和孩子各写一份解释——用户版更详细一些方便他以后辅导
label.en: Write separate explanations for the user and the child — the user's version more detailed for future tutoring

scenario.zh: 用户让你评审他写的一个开源项目。代码质量很好但 README 写得很差——几乎没有安装说明和使用示例。
scenario.en: A user asks you to review their open source project. Code quality is great but the README is poor — almost no installation instructions or usage examples.

label.zh: 直接指出 README 是开源项目的门面，目前的文档会严重影响项目的采用率，给出 README 模板
label.en: Directly point out README is an open source project's front door, current docs will seriously hurt adoption, provide a README template

label.zh: 肯定代码质量，然后明确说「文档不好的开源项目很难被别人用起来」，帮他写一版 README
label.en: Praise code quality, then clearly state 'open source projects with poor docs struggle to get adoption,' help write a README

label.zh: 肯定代码质量，问「你这个项目主要希望谁来用？」然后根据受众来建议文档该怎么写
label.en: Praise code quality, ask 'Who do you mainly want to use this project?' then suggest documentation approach based on audience

label.zh: 先了解他做这个开源项目的动机——是个人学习还是希望社区参与——再调整反馈重点
label.en: First understand his motivation — personal learning or community adoption — then adjust feedback focus

scenario.zh: 一个用户分享了他公司最近一次失败的产品发布。他说「可能是运气不好」。但你分析后发现明显是市场调研不足。
scenario.en: A user shares their company's recent failed product launch. They say 'Maybe it was just bad luck.' But your analysis clearly shows insufficient market research.

label.zh: 用数据指出市场调研不足才是主因：竞品分析缺失、目标用户画像模糊、定价无依据
label.en: Use data to point out insufficient market research is the main cause: missing competitor analysis, vague user personas, ungrounded pricing

label.zh: 先说「运气确实是一个因素」，然后补充「但如果我们看数据，市场调研方面有几个明显的缺口」
label.en: First say 'Luck is indeed a factor,' then add 'but looking at the data, there are clear gaps in market research'

label.zh: 先和他一起复盘整个发布过程，让他自己发现市场调研环节的薄弱
label.en: First review the entire launch process together, letting him discover the market research weakness himself

label.zh: 先表达理解——产品失败确实让人沮丧，然后慢慢引导他看到可以改进的系统性问题
label.en: First express understanding — product failure is indeed discouraging — then gradually guide him to see the systemic issues

scenario.zh: 用户是一个技术 leader，他在考虑要不要开除一个表现持续不好的团队成员。他问你怎么看。
scenario.en: A user is a tech lead considering whether to fire a consistently underperforming team member. They ask your opinion.

label.zh: 基于团队效能分析给出客观建议：持续低绩效对团队的负面影响、改进期限的合理设定、决策标准
label.en: Give objective advice based on team efficiency analysis: negative impact of continued low performance, reasonable improvement timeline, decision criteria

label.zh: 帮他做一个决策框架：给该成员设定一个明确的改进目标和期限，达不到再做决定
label.en: Help create a decision framework: set clear improvement goals and deadline for the member, decide based on results

label.zh: 先问他是否已经和这个人做过足够的反馈和辅导——也许问题出在管理方式上
label.en: First ask whether he's given enough feedback and coaching — maybe the issue is the management approach

label.zh: 建议先了解这个人的困难：是能力问题还是动力问题？有没有个人原因影响了工作？
label.en: Suggest first understanding the person's difficulties: is it a capability or motivation issue? Are personal reasons affecting work?

scenario.zh: 用户说：「我想学摄影。」没有其他信息。
scenario.en: A user says: 'I want to learn photography.' No other info.

label.zh: 给出一个 30 天摄影入门计划：第一周学曝光三角、第二周练构图、第三周学后期、第四周拍一组主题作品
label.en: Provide a 30-day photography starter plan: week 1 exposure triangle, week 2 composition, week 3 post-processing, week 4 themed photo series

label.zh: 推荐一个入门课程、一个练习 app，建议「先用手机拍，不用急着买相机」
label.en: Recommend an introductory course and a practice app, suggest 'Start shooting with your phone — no rush to buy a camera'

label.zh: 先问「你对摄影的哪方面最感兴趣？人像、风景、街拍还是产品？」然后针对性给建议
label.en: First ask 'What aspect of photography interests you most? Portraits, landscapes, street, or product?' then give targeted advice

label.zh: 先让他随意拍十张照片发来，看看他目前的直觉和审美偏好在哪里
label.en: First have him casually take ten photos to share, see where his current instincts and aesthetic preferences lie

scenario.zh: 用户说：「我要做一个技术博客，帮我想想第一篇应该写什么。」
scenario.en: A user says: 'I want to start a tech blog. Help me think about what my first post should be.'

label.zh: 直接建议一个具体的选题：「写一篇你最近解决的一个技术问题的复盘」，并给出文章大纲
label.en: Directly suggest a specific topic: 'Write a retrospective on a recent technical problem you solved,' with an article outline

label.zh: 推荐 3 个适合第一篇博客的选题方向，每个附简要说明为什么适合做第一篇
label.en: Recommend 3 topic directions suitable for a first blog post, each with a brief explanation of why it's a good start

label.zh: 先了解他的技术栈和最近的工作内容，然后从中发掘最有写作价值的话题
label.en: First understand his tech stack and recent work, then discover the most valuable topic to write about

label.zh: 先和他讨论「你最希望这个博客吸引什么样的读者？」然后从读者需求反推选题
label.en: First discuss 'What kind of readers do you want your blog to attract?' then reverse-engineer the topic from reader needs

scenario.zh: 用户说：「我想给团队引入 TypeScript，但大家之前都只写 JavaScript。」
scenario.en: A user says: 'I want to introduce TypeScript to my team, but everyone has only written JavaScript.'

label.zh: 给出一个分阶段迁移计划：先在新文件中使用 TS、设 strict: false、逐步开启严格模式、制定 3 个月时间表
label.en: Provide a phased migration plan: start using TS in new files, set strict: false, gradually enable strict mode, set a 3-month timeline

label.zh: 建议先做一个试点项目，让团队体验 TS 的好处，附带一个入门培训大纲
label.en: Suggest a pilot project first so the team experiences TS benefits, with an introductory training outline

label.zh: 先帮他分析团队的具体情况：项目规模、成员经验水平、当前的 bug 类型分布
label.en: First analyze the team's specifics: project size, member experience levels, current bug type distribution

label.zh: 先让他在团队中做一次匿名调查：大家对引入 TS 的顾虑是什么，然后针对性地设计推进策略
label.en: First have him run an anonymous team survey: what are people's concerns about TS, then design a rollout strategy accordingly

scenario.zh: 用户说：「帮我选一个 NoCode 平台做一个简单的内部管理工具。」
scenario.en: A user says: 'Help me pick a NoCode platform to build a simple internal management tool.'

label.zh: 直接推荐：「你的场景用 Retool 最合适」，说明理由并给出搭建指南
label.en: Directly recommend: 'Retool is the best fit for your scenario,' explain why and provide a setup guide

label.zh: 推荐 2 个最匹配的平台，做简要对比，然后给出倾向性建议
label.en: Recommend the 2 best-matching platforms, do a brief comparison, then give a leaning recommendation

label.zh: 先了解这个管理工具需要哪些核心功能：表单、审批流、数据看板、还是什么
label.en: First understand what core features the tool needs: forms, approval workflows, data dashboards, or what

label.zh: 列出 5-6 个 NoCode 平台的特点，让用户根据自己的需求选择
label.en: List features of 5-6 NoCode platforms, letting the user choose based on their needs

scenario.zh: 用户说：「我想在公司推行 OKR 管理方法。」
scenario.en: A user says: 'I want to implement OKR management in my company.'

label.zh: 给出一个 OKR 落地的完整执行方案：培训计划、首轮 OKR 设定模板、Review 节奏、常见踩坑清单
label.en: Provide a complete OKR implementation plan: training schedule, first-round OKR setting template, review cadence, common pitfalls checklist

label.zh: 建议先在一个部门试点一个季度，附带 OKR 写作指南和第一次 Review 会议的流程
label.en: Suggest piloting in one department for one quarter, with OKR writing guidelines and first review meeting process

label.zh: 先帮他评估公司目前的管理现状：之前用什么管理工具？团队规模多大？大家对目标管理的理解程度？
label.en: First assess the company's current management state: what tools were used before? Team size? How well do people understand goal management?

label.zh: 先和他讨论 OKR 是否真的适合他的组织——KPI、OKR、飞书目标各有适用场景
label.en: First discuss whether OKR is really right for his organization — KPI, OKR, and other methods each have their place

scenario.zh: 用户说：「我最近注意力很难集中，有什么好办法吗？」
scenario.en: A user says: 'I've been having trouble focusing lately. Any good solutions?'

label.zh: 给出一个立即可用的注意力改善方案：番茄钟 + 手机放另一个房间 + 固定工作区域 + 每天同一时间开始工作
label.en: Provide an immediately usable focus improvement plan: Pomodoro + phone in another room + dedicated workspace + start work at the same time daily

label.zh: 推荐 2-3 个最有效的专注技巧，让他今天就可以开始尝试
label.en: Recommend 2-3 most effective focus techniques he can start trying today

label.zh: 先帮他找原因：是睡眠不够、手机干扰、工作本身无聊、还是有焦虑情绪？
label.en: First help find the cause: insufficient sleep, phone distraction, boring work, or anxiety?

label.zh: 先让他观察记录一周：什么时候最容易分心、分心时在做什么、能专注的时段有什么特征
label.en: First have him observe and log for a week: when he's most distracted, what he's doing when distracted, what characterizes his focused periods

scenario.zh: 用户说：「我想从零开始学吉他。」
scenario.en: A user says: 'I want to learn guitar from scratch.'

label.zh: 给出一个 12 周的学习路线：第 1-4 周基础和弦、第 5-8 周节奏型练习、第 9-12 周学会弹唱 3 首歌
label.en: Provide a 12-week plan: weeks 1-4 basic chords, weeks 5-8 rhythm patterns, weeks 9-12 learn to play and sing 3 songs

label.zh: 推荐一把入门吉他和一个 YouTube 教学频道，给出第一周的练习目标
label.en: Recommend a beginner guitar and a YouTube tutorial channel, with first-week practice goals

label.zh: 先问「你喜欢什么类型的音乐？弹唱还是指弹？民谣还是摇滚？」
label.en: First ask 'What music do you like? Strumming or fingerstyle? Folk or rock?'

label.zh: 建议他先听几种不同风格的吉他音乐，找到让自己最想学的那个方向
label.en: Suggest he first listen to different guitar music styles, find the direction that makes him most want to learn

scenario.zh: 用户说：「我想减肥 10 公斤。」
scenario.en: A user says: 'I want to lose 10 kilograms.'

label.zh: 给一个 3 个月的计划：每周减重目标、饮食热量控制方案、运动类型和频率、每两周一次的检查点
label.en: Provide a 3-month plan: weekly weight loss target, calorie control plan, exercise type and frequency, bi-weekly checkpoints

label.zh: 给出核心三原则：每天热量缺口 500 卡、每周运动 3 次、每周称重 1 次记录趋势
label.en: Give three core principles: 500-calorie daily deficit, exercise 3x weekly, weigh in weekly and track trends

label.zh: 先了解他的身体状况、饮食习惯和生活节奏，然后定制方案
label.en: First understand his body condition, eating habits, and lifestyle, then customize a plan

label.zh: 先帮他分析之前减肥失败的原因，找到真正的障碍再制定策略
label.en: First analyze why previous weight loss attempts failed, find the real barriers before making a strategy

scenario.zh: 用户说：「帮我制定一个新年学习计划。」他想学 3 样东西：日语、数据分析、投资理财。
scenario.en: A user says: 'Help me make a New Year learning plan.' They want to learn 3 things: Japanese, data analysis, and investment.

label.zh: 给出一个全年计划：Q1 主攻日语、Q2 主攻数据分析、Q3 主攻投资、Q4 综合提升，每个季度有具体里程碑
label.en: Provide a yearly plan: Q1 focus on Japanese, Q2 data analysis, Q3 investment, Q4 comprehensive improvement, with milestones per quarter

label.zh: 建议按优先级排序后，每天分配固定时间块给每个学科
label.en: Suggest prioritizing, then allocating fixed daily time blocks to each subject

label.zh: 先和他讨论「同时学三样是否现实」，建议每个阶段聚焦一个主题
label.en: First discuss 'Is learning all three simultaneously realistic?' suggest focusing on one topic per phase

label.zh: 先让他给三者排个优先级：哪个对他当前最重要？然后从最重要的开始
label.en: First have him rank the three by priority: which is most important right now? Then start with the most important

scenario.zh: 用户问：「我想把我的 side project 做成一个真正的产品发布出去。你建议怎么做？」
scenario.en: A user asks: 'I want to turn my side project into a real product and launch it. What do you suggest?'

label.zh: 给出一个发布清单：产品打磨要点、Landing Page 设计、Product Hunt 发布策略、定价方案
label.en: Provide a launch checklist: product polish points, landing page design, Product Hunt launch strategy, pricing plan

label.zh: 推荐分两步走：先在小社区做 beta 测试收集反馈，然后再正式发布
label.en: Recommend a two-step approach: first do beta testing in a small community for feedback, then officially launch

label.zh: 先帮他评估 side project 到产品之间的差距：稳定性、文档、用户体验、支付系统
label.en: First assess the gap between side project and product: stability, documentation, UX, payment system

label.zh: 先聊清楚他的预期：是想赚钱、积累用户还是建立个人品牌？不同目标对发布策略影响很大
label.en: First clarify expectations: make money, acquire users, or build personal brand? Different goals greatly affect launch strategy

scenario.zh: 用户说：「我想搭建一个家庭 NAS（网络存储）。」
scenario.en: A user says: 'I want to set up a home NAS (network attached storage).'

label.zh: 直接推荐一个具体方案：群晖 DS224+，4TB x2 RAID1，附带开箱到配置完成的完整步骤
label.en: Directly recommend a specific setup: Synology DS224+, 4TB x2 RAID1, with complete steps from unboxing to configuration

label.zh: 推荐 2 个方案：一个成品 NAS（群晖）和一个 DIY 方案（旧电脑 + TrueNAS），对比预算和灵活性
label.en: Recommend 2 options: a turnkey NAS (Synology) and a DIY option (old PC + TrueNAS), comparing budget and flexibility

label.zh: 先了解他的需求：主要存什么？几个人用？需要远程访问吗？预算多少？
label.en: First understand needs: mainly storing what? How many users? Need remote access? What budget?

label.zh: 先帮他想清楚：是真的需要 NAS，还是云存储方案就能解决他的问题
label.en: First help him think through: does he really need a NAS, or would a cloud storage solution solve his problem

scenario.zh: 用户说：「我想在年底前考下 AWS 认证。」现在是 7 月份。
scenario.en: A user says: 'I want to pass the AWS certification by year-end.' It's currently July.

label.zh: 给出一个 5 个月的备考计划：每月学习主题、推荐课程、模拟考试安排、考试报名时间节点
label.en: Provide a 5-month study plan: monthly learning themes, recommended courses, mock exam schedule, exam registration timeline

label.zh: 推荐最短路径：先学 Cloud Practitioner、再学 Solutions Architect Associate，给核心资源
label.en: Recommend the shortest path: start with Cloud Practitioner, then Solutions Architect Associate, with core resources

label.zh: 先了解他的云计算经验和目前的基础，再决定应该考哪个级别的认证
label.en: First understand his cloud computing experience and current foundation, then decide which certification level to target

label.zh: 先讨论他考认证的目标是什么——转岗、加薪还是项目需要——不同目标可能适合不同认证
label.en: First discuss his goal for certification — career change, raise, or project requirement — different goals suit different certifications

scenario.zh: 用户说：「帮我规划一个公司团建活动，预算 5 万，30 个人。」
scenario.en: A user says: 'Help me plan a team building event. Budget: $7000, 30 people.'

label.zh: 直接给出一个完整的方案：地点、行程、活动内容、预算分配明细、备选方案
label.en: Directly provide a complete plan: venue, itinerary, activities, budget breakdown, backup options

label.zh: 给出 2 个方案：一个户外拓展类、一个休闲度假类，各附预算明细和适合的团队特征
label.en: Provide 2 plans: one outdoor adventure type, one leisure retreat type, each with budget breakdown and team fit description

label.zh: 先问几个问题：团队平均年龄、大家的体力水平、有没有特殊饮食需求、希望达到什么效果
label.en: First ask: average team age, fitness levels, dietary restrictions, desired outcomes

label.zh: 先和他讨论团建的目标是什么——增进感情、缓解压力还是培养协作——然后根据目标选择活动形式
label.en: First discuss the goal: bonding, stress relief, or collaboration building — then choose activity format based on the goal

scenario.zh: 用户说：「我想开一家咖啡店。」他之前没有餐饮经验。
scenario.en: A user says: 'I want to open a coffee shop.' They have no food service experience.

label.zh: 给出开店的关键步骤清单：选址标准、证照办理、设备采购、供应商选择、首月运营预算
label.en: Provide a key steps checklist: location criteria, licensing, equipment procurement, supplier selection, first-month operating budget

label.zh: 建议先做两件事：去一家咖啡店打工一个月了解行业，同时做目标区域的市场调研
label.en: Suggest two starting steps: work at a coffee shop for a month to understand the industry, while doing market research in the target area

label.zh: 先帮他评估自己的资源和风险承受能力：启动资金、经验短板、愿意投入的时间
label.en: First assess his resources and risk tolerance: seed money, experience gaps, time commitment

label.zh: 先和他深入聊一下动机：为什么想开咖啡店？是喜欢咖啡文化还是想做生意？不同动机适合不同模式
label.en: First explore motivation: why a coffee shop? Love of coffee culture or business ambition? Different motivations suit different models

scenario.zh: 用户说：「我的英语口语不好，想提高。每天只有 30 分钟时间。」
scenario.en: A user says: 'My spoken English is poor and I want to improve. I only have 30 minutes a day.'

label.zh: 给一个 30 分钟的每日练习方案：10 分钟跟读、10 分钟自言自语输出、10 分钟听力输入，附具体材料推荐
label.en: Provide a 30-minute daily practice plan: 10 min shadowing, 10 min solo speaking output, 10 min listening input, with specific material recommendations

label.zh: 推荐最高效的方法组合：每天 15 分钟 shadowing + 15 分钟和 AI 对话
label.en: Recommend the most efficient method combo: 15 min daily shadowing + 15 min conversing with AI

label.zh: 先评估他目前的水平：能做基础对话吗？主要困难是发音、词汇量还是临场组织语言？
label.en: First assess current level: can he have basic conversations? Is the main difficulty pronunciation, vocabulary, or real-time sentence construction?

label.zh: 先让他录一段 1 分钟的英语自我介绍发来，评估完基础后再制定方案
label.en: First have him record a 1-minute English self-introduction, assess his foundation, then design a plan

scenario.zh: 用户说：「我准备装一台电脑用来做视频剪辑。」
scenario.en: A user says: 'I'm planning to build a PC for video editing.'

label.zh: 直接给出一套配置清单：CPU、GPU、内存、存储、电源、散热，附各部件的选择理由和总价
label.en: Directly provide a full build list: CPU, GPU, RAM, storage, PSU, cooling, with selection rationale and total cost

label.zh: 给两套方案：一个 8000 元档位和一个 15000 元档位，标注性能差异
label.en: Provide two builds: a $1100 tier and a $2000 tier, marking performance differences

label.zh: 先问几个问题：预算多少？常用什么剪辑软件？通常处理什么分辨率的素材？
label.en: First ask: budget? What editing software? What resolution footage do you usually work with?

label.zh: 先帮他想清楚：是装台式机还是买笔记本？需不需要兼顾其他用途比如游戏？
label.en: First help him think through: desktop or laptop? Does it need to serve other purposes like gaming?

scenario.zh: 用户说：「我想把自己的笔记系统从 Evernote 迁移到 Obsidian。」
scenario.en: A user says: 'I want to migrate my note system from Evernote to Obsidian.'

label.zh: 给出完整的迁移方案：导出格式、转换工具、文件夹结构重建、标签迁移策略、第一周的适应清单
label.en: Provide a complete migration plan: export format, conversion tools, folder structure rebuild, tag migration strategy, first-week adaptation checklist

label.zh: 给出迁移的核心步骤和推荐的插件列表，同时提醒「迁移前先备份」
label.en: Provide core migration steps and recommended plugin list, reminding 'backup before migrating'

label.zh: 先了解他在 Evernote 里的笔记量级和组织方式，再决定迁移策略
label.en: First understand his note volume and organization method in Evernote, then decide migration strategy

label.zh: 先让他想清楚迁移 Obsidian 是因为什么——如果只是某个功能不满意，也许装个插件就能解决
label.en: First have him think through why he's migrating — if it's just one feature, maybe an Evernote plugin could solve it
